mnemonics.title: C KEYWORDS MEMORIZATION
mnemonics.content:
A S I F R E G I S T E R V O L A T I L E
Auto Static If For Return Extern Goto Register Volatile
C H A R D O U B L E L O N G S I G N E D S H O R T U N I O N
Char Double Long Signed Short Union
C O N S T E N U M S I Z E O F S T R U C T
Const Enum Sizeof Struct
S W I T C H C A S E D E F A U L T B R E A K C O N T I N U E
Switch Case Default Break Continue
W H I L E D O E L S E
While Do Else
E X T E R N R E T U R N V O I D
Extern Return Void

mnemonics.title: CONST VS DEFINE
mnemonics.content:
CONST: COMPILER-ENFORCED, TYPE-CHECKED, HAS SCOPE
DEFINE: PREPROCESSOR SUBSTITUTION, NO TYPE CHECK, FILE-SCOPE
Mnemonic: CONST = COMPILATION TIME, DEFINE = PRE-PROCESSING TIME

mnemonics.title: INTEGER SUFFIXES
mnemonics.content:
U or u = UNSIGNED
L or l = LONG
UL = UNSIGNED LONG
LL = LONG LONG (C99)
Mnemonic: U IS FOR UNSIGNED, L IS FOR LONGER, LL IS DOUBLE LONG

acronyms.usage: Used to define symbolic constants: #define MAX 100

acronyms.expansion: Constant Qualifier - Declares Variables with Fixed Values
acronyms.usage: Used with const keyword: const int x = 10;

acronyms.expansion: Enumeration - User-Defined Set of Integer Constants
acronyms.usage: Creates named integer constants: enum days {MON,TUE,WED};

acronyms.expansion: American Standard Code for Information Interchange

acronyms.usage: ANSI C defines the standard 32 keywords in C language

visualTips.visualization: Think of enum as a staircase where each step increases by 1. First value is initial value, each subsequent value is previous +1. Can set custom starting points, but still increment.
Read declaration from right to left: int *const ptr = constant pointer to int; const int *ptr = pointer to constant int.
#define is like a rubber stamp - it stamps the same value everywhere before the code is compiled. const is like a locked box - it stores a value that cannot be changed once set.

analogies.analogy: Constants are like the speed of light - it is a fixed value that doesn't change. In programs, you use constants for values that should never change, like PI (3.14159), maximum limits, or tax rates.
Keywords are like reserved seats in a theater. Once reserved, regular customers (variables) cannot use them. They are exclusively for the language's use.
Symbolic constants are like nicknames. Instead of remembering 3.14159265359 everywhere, you just use PI. If you ever need to change it, you change it in one place.
Enum is like labeling days of the week. Instead of remembering Monday=0, Tuesday=1, etc., you just use the names and C handles the numbers internally.
